LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAR-03-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS DAVID LAU AND PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR. THE PARTIES FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 430.41. THE PARTIES ARE IMMEDIATELY ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE CODE. DEFENDANTS' RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY MARCH 31, 2021. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Supporting Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Supporting Declaration Of Jewell J. Hargleroad With Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Brett D. Schweinberg (415-673-5600; bschweinberg@g3mh.com) of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP for Plaintiff.
Jewell Hargleroad (510-331-2975; jewellhargleroad@mac.com) for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS DAVID LAU and PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint:

Defendants' motion to strike "false" statements is denied. Defendants' arguments raise disputed issues of fact inappropriate for resolution on a motion to strike. "In passing on the correctness of a ruling on a motion to strike, judges read allegations of a pleading subject to a motion to strike as a whole, all parts in their context, and assume their truth." (Clauson v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1253, 1255; see also PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683 ["We emphasize that such use of the motion to strike should be cautious and sparing. We have no intention of creating a procedural 'line item veto' for the civil defendant."].) Plaintiffs are granted leave to file an amendment to the FAC correcting the admitted $5,000 error in paragraphs 14 and 15 (compare Settlement Agreement, Section III(2)(a).)

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS DAVID LAU AND PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is off calendar. The parties failed to meet and confer in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41. The parties are immediately ordered to meet and confer in compliance with the Code. Defendants' response to the amended complaint is now due by March 31, 2021.
